blockers.description: src/ibkr_trader/state/store.py:69-73 (upsert_position, schema at store.py:50 `symbol TEXT PRIMARY KEY`) -> `store.upsert_position(265598, "AAPL", 10)` followed by `store.upsert_position(272093, "AAPL", 5)` (two distinct conIds, same display symbol) raises `sqlite3.IntegrityError: UNIQUE constraint failed: positions.symbol`; the DELETE removes nothing (existing row has non-null con_id) and the `ON CONFLICT(con_id)` clause does not cover the symbol-PK conflict. Same crash also hits the symbol-reassignment path — e.g. `upsert_position(272093,"MSFT",-4)`, `upsert_position(265598,"AAPL",10)`, then `upsert_position(272093,"AAPL",7)` — where the `ON CONFLICT(con_id) DO UPDATE SET symbol='AAPL'` violates the existing AAPL primary key.

src/ibkr_trader/state/store.py:52-57 (StateStore._init_schema) -> two StateStore instances (two autopilot processes) constructed concurrently against an existing legacy-schema DB (positions table without a con_id column) both execute `PRAGMA table_info(positions)` seeing no con_id before either commits its `ALTER TABLE positions ADD COLUMN con_id`; the second ALTER then raises sqlite3.OperationalError: duplicate column name: con_id — the check-then-ALTER is non-atomic and unguarded (no IF NOT EXISTS), and the per-instance threading.Lock does not serialize across processes.

confirmed: src/ibkr_trader/state/store.py:69-73 (upsert_position, schema at store.py:50 `symbol TEXT PRIMARY KEY`) -> `store.upsert_position(265598, "AAPL", 10)` followed by `store.upsert_position(272093, "AAPL", 5)` (two distinct conIds, same display symbol) raises `sqlite3.IntegrityError: UNIQUE constraint failed: positions.symbol`; the DELETE removes nothing (existing row has non-null con_id) and the `ON CONFLICT(con_id)` clause does not cover the symbol-PK conflict. Same crash also hits the symbol-reassignment path — e.g. `upsert_position(272093,"MSFT",-4)`, `upsert_position(265598,"AAPL",10)`, then `upsert_position(272093,"AAPL",7)` — where the `ON CONFLICT(con_id) DO UPDATE SET symbol='AAPL'` violates the existing AAPL primary key.

src/ibkr_trader/state/store.py:52-57 (StateStore._init_schema) -> two StateStore instances (two autopilot processes) constructed concurrently against an existing legacy-schema DB (positions table without a con_id column) both execute `PRAGMA table_info(positions)` seeing no con_id before either commits its `ALTER TABLE positions ADD COLUMN con_id`; the second ALTER then raises sqlite3.OperationalError: duplicate column name: con_id — the check-then-ALTER is non-atomic and unguarded (no IF NOT EXISTS), and the per-instance threading.Lock does not serialize across processes.
refuted: src/ibkr_trader/state/store.py:69-73 (StateStore.upsert_position) -> upsert_position(1, "ES", 10) then upsert_position(2, "ES", 10) (same display symbol re-resolved to a new conId — futures roll / ticker reuse / two concurrent autopilots disagreeing on the conId for a symbol) raises sqlite3.IntegrityError: UNIQUE constraint failed: positions.symbol, because the table declares `symbol TEXT PRIMARY KEY` and `ON CONFLICT(con_id)` never handles the symbol-PK collision (the leading DELETE only clears rows with con_id IS NULL). The store cannot hold two contracts that ever shared a display symbol — the exact conId-identity guarantee PT-4a claims.
